stimulus: In 1872, Japanese Emperor Meiji issued a decree dissolving the centuries-old samurai class, and many former retainers found themselves stripped of their stipends. Determined to restore his fortunes after years of poverty, ______blank eventually becoming one of the most prosperous tea exporters in the Shizuoka region.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

answerOptions.content: the tea fields of Nakamura were cultivated,
the cultivation of tea fields by Nakamura began,
it was Nakamura who cultivated the tea fields,

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The convention being tested is subject-modifier placement. This choice ensures that the introductory phrase “Determined to restore his fortunes after years of poverty” appears immediately before the noun it modifies (“Nakamura”), clearly establishing that Nakamura is the one determined to restore his fortunes.

Choice A is incorrect because it results in a dangling modifier. The placement of the noun phrase “the tea fields of Nakamura” immediately after the introductory phrase illogically suggests that the tea fields were determined to restore his fortunes. Choice C is incorrect because it results in a dangling modifier. The placement of the noun phrase “the cultivation of tea fields” immediately after the introductory phrase illogically suggests that the cultivation was determined to restore his fortunes. Choice D is incorrect because it results in a dangling modifier. The placement of the function word “it” immediately after the introductory phrase illogically suggests that “it” was determined to restore his fortunes.
